assertion_results.text: The unsupported ecosystem notification uses the detected ecosystem name ('Go modules') — not a hard-coded ecosystem name (Step 5.3 unsupported ecosystem handling)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 29 detects ecosystem as 'Go modules'. unsupported-ecosystem.md line 3 uses that same name: 'Go modules is not yet supported for automated triage.' The notification uses the detected ecosystem name consistently.

assertion_results.text: The notification follows the template pattern: the message contains the actual ecosystem name substituted into the placeholder, matching the angle-bracket placeholder convention used by other user-facing messages in the skill
assertion_results.evidence: unsupported-ecosystem.md line 3 reads 'Go modules is not yet supported for automated triage. Manual assessment is required.' The actual ecosystem name 'Go modules' is substituted into the message text, not left as a raw placeholder like '<ecosystem>'.

assertion_results.text: Automated triage stops after the unsupported ecosystem notification — no version impact analysis or remediation task creation is performed for the unsupported ecosystem
assertion_results.evidence: Only two output files exist: data-extraction.md and unsupported-ecosystem.md. No version impact analysis or remediation task files are present. unsupported-ecosystem.md line 15 explicitly states: 'Automated triage has stopped at Step 1 (Ecosystem Detection). No version impact analysis, Affects Versions correction, or remediation task creation has been performed.'

assertion_results.text: The notification includes both the ecosystem name and the instruction that manual assessment is required
assertion_results.evidence: unsupported-ecosystem.md line 3: 'Go modules is not yet supported for automated triage. Manual assessment is required.' Contains ecosystem name ('Go modules') and manual assessment instruction ('Manual assessment is required'). Also reiterated in data-extraction.md line 37: 'Manual assessment is required.'
